title: AI Lab Journey
subtitle: Telkom University AI Laboratory
reflection: Teaching 29 students weekly forced me to understand ML concepts properly. You cannot bluff a question you did not expect.
I reviewed 70+ applicants for the study group. What I was actually looking for: people who ask follow-up questions, not people who already know the answers.
I learned that a coordinator's job is different from a member's job. As a member, you execute. As a coordinator, you make sure other people can execute.
Supervising 15 project groups showed me how many ways a project can be technically running and scientifically meaningless at the same time.
I learned to evaluate research quality: not just whether the code runs, but whether the question was worth asking.

highlights: First year in AI Lab. Joined as a member, got assigned team lead on the plant disease classifier project.
Built the full pipeline in Python: data cleaning, training DL model for computer vision, Streamlit deployment. Done in one semester.
Won 3rd place at CCI Summit Kaggle competition the same year, first competition as team lead.

milestones.role: Study Group Coordinator · Focused Group Member
highlights: Promoted to coordinator. Now responsible for 29 students every week. Not just doing the work, but explaining it.
Ran recruitment: 70+ applicants, filtered down to a cohort. Harder than it sounds.
Joined the Focused Group, the research track inside AI Lab for members doing active projects.
Supervised 15 project groups from proposal to demo. Saw every way a project can go wrong.
Now running the Focused Group, the advanced research track.
Setting research direction, reviewing member output, connecting projects to real-world partners.
AI Lab work feeds into active industry collaborations and thesis research.
